channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Meet four Minnesota federal workers mourning the loss of their dream jobs
item.description: Many Minnesotans are among those facing unemployment, as the Trump administration slashes jobs across agencies. The state is home to more than 18,000 federal workers, with nearly 60 percent working for the Department of Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: NRSC chair reveals how many GOP Senate seats he's gunning for during 2026 midterms
item.description: National Republican Senatorial Committee Chair Tim Scott aims to expand the GOP's majority in the chamber in the 2026 midterm elections and thanks President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump’s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A federal judge has largely blocked sweeping executive orders from President Donald Trump that sought to end government support for programs promoting diversity, equity and inclusion.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration reassigns acting ICE director
item.description: The Trump administration is replacing the acting head of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) amid simmering from some officials about deportation numbers. A Department of Homeland Security official confirmed that Caleb Vitello was being reassigned within the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican senators pushed a $340 billion budget framework to passage early Friday, chugging through an all-night session and Democratic opposition in a step toward unleashing money the Trump administration says it needs for mass deportations and border security that top their agen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Hochul won't remove NYC Mayor Eric Adams, but pushes to limit powers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will remain in office as Gov. Kathy Hochul pushes to implement "certain guardrails" to help protect New Yorkers. Hochul cited the “democratic process” as why she chose not to remove Adams from office.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers to Trump: Reverse cuts to 9/11 health care
item.description: New York's senate and congressional delegations agree that federal cuts to the World Trade Center Health Program will hurt survivors of and first responders to the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, PIX 11

item.title: Walz family starts to make a move
item.description: After a nearly two-year renovation of the governor’s residence, the Summit Avenue mansion is ready to serve as the first family’s living quarters again.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: IRS to lay off 6,700 probationary workers
item.description: The IRS now tops the agency with the most probationary separations during the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Trump hosts event honoring Black History Month
item.description: The celebration comes amid the administration's moves to gut federal diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs and place DEI employees on leave.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump moves hamper bird flu response as egg prices spike
item.description: When President Trump took office, his administration instituted an external communications blackout across health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The chorus, one of the oldest LGBTQ choral organizations in the U.S., was set to perform alongside the National Symphony Orchestra in May.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month in: Survey
item.description: The CNN poll shows that while 47 percent of adults in the U.S. say they approve of Trump's moves since returning to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vow to preserve Medicaid collides with House GOP plan for tax cuts
item.description: President Trump’s vow to keep Medicaid intact is colliding headlong into the Republicans’ strategy to extend their 2017 tax cuts, complicating the GOP effort to secure a major policy win in the early months of Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Black farmers among those left in limbo amid federal funding freeze
item.description: The federal government has historically discriminated against Black farmers. The recent funding halt from the Trump administration presents yet another hurdle to those who have spent decades fighting for equity in farming.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration yanks CDC flu vaccine campaign
item.description: As flu rages, the Trump administration has pulled the plug on a Centers for Disease Control and Prevention flu immunization campaign that targeted high-risk groups, including pregnant women.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota’s site as a world hockey showcase comes with request for state aid for tourney
item.description: Local organizers are seeking $5 million in state funds toward staging the 2026 World Junior Hockey Championship next year.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota bill seeks to disable AI tech used to create nude or sexual images of people
item.description: The plan would require artificial intelligence companies to deactivate a feature that allows users to make people appear naked or engaged in sexual acts in photos or videos without their consent.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Nina Moini, and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Nevada lawmakers target 'resort fee'-style rent practices, age for gun ownership
item.description: Hidden fees are so out-of-hand that it now costs money just to pay the rent. And it's a good bet that tenants aren't told that until they have signed the lease.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5
